nodes.name: 鹮科
nodes.summary: 鹮科（Threskiornithidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联30种鸟类，如朱鹮、黑脸琵鹭、Plumbeous ibis等30种鸟类。

nodes.name: 白琵鹭
nodes.summary: 白琵鹭（Platalea leucorodia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如白琵鷺。

nodes.name: 湿地排水
nodes.summary: 湿地被排干转为农业或建设用地。

nodes.name: 污染
nodes.summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

nodes.name: 卡尔·林奈
nodes.summary: 卡尔·林奈是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联115种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、角叫鴨、白臉樹鴨等115种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 琵鹭属
nodes.summary: 琵鹭属（Platalea）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联6种鸟类，如黑脸琵鹭、玫瑰琵鷺、黃嘴琵鷺等6种鸟类。

nodes.name: 白琵鷺
nodes.summary: 白琵鷺（Eurasian spoonbill，学名 Platalea leucorodia），分类上属于鹈形目，并归入鹮科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、污染、湿地排水等4项。
nodes.shortSummary: 白琵鹭（學名：Platalea leucorodia），屬於鹮科琵鹭属。 生态环境 喜欢在泥泞水塘、湖泊或泥滩等生境中活动，常在水中缓慢前进，嘴往两旁甩动以寻找食物。

nodes.name: 伊比利亚半岛
nodes.summary: 伊比利亚半岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括黑腹沙鸡、普通潜鸟、白琵鷺等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鹈形目
nodes.summary: 鹈形目（Pelecaniformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联93种鸟类，如朱鹮、黑脸琵鹭、Plumbeous ibis等93种鸟类。

nodes.name: 朝鮮 (地區)
nodes.summary: 朝鮮 (地區)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联39种鸟类，包括丹顶鹤、朱鹮、黑琴鸡等39种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 自然系统第十版
nodes.summary: 自然系统第十版是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联64种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、黑嘴樹鴨、加拿大雁等64种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Eurasian spoonbill (Platalea leucorodia), or common spoonbill, is a wading bird of the ibis and spoonbill family Threskiornithidae, native to Europe, Africa and Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The Eurasian spoonbill (Platalea leucorodia), or common spoonbill, is a wading bird of the ibis and spoonbill family Threskiornithidae, native to Europe, Africa and Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In Asia, it breeds in a broad band across the central part of the continent, from the Black Sea to the Korean Peninsula, as well as Kuwait, southern Iraq, Iran, southern Pakistan, India and Sri Lanka

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Whereas those breeding in warmer parts of Asia, in Africa and the Iberian Peninsula are resident or only move locally, more northern breeders generally migrate south to winter in southwestern Europe, the northern half of Africa or warm parts of Asia

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: In 2011, 8 breeding pairs nested, successfully fledging 14 young, and in 2018 the colony had increased to 28 breeding pairs. Threats to the Eurasian spoonbill include habitat loss and degradation by drainage and pollution, it is especially adversely affected by the disappearance of reed swamps

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: In 2011, 8 breeding pairs nested, successfully fledging 14 young, and in 2018 the colony had increased to 28 breeding pairs. Threats to the Eurasian spoonbill include habitat loss and degradation by drainage and pollution, it is especially adversely affected by the disappearance of reed swamps

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: In 2011, 8 breeding pairs nested, successfully fledging 14 young, and in 2018 the colony had increased to 28 breeding pairs. Threats to the Eurasian spoonbill include habitat loss and degradation by drainage and pollution, it is especially adversely affected by the disappearance of reed swamps

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: In Greece disturbance from fishing once caused the population to decline, and human exploitation of eggs and nestlings for food has threatened the species in the past. Breeding colonies are highly vulnerable to general disturbances and predators like red fox. Consequently, colonies are often restricted to islands free of ground predators; however, in some places, pairs in mainland colonies may breed successfully by nesting off the ground in trees and bushes. The research network Eurasian Spoonbill International Expert Group was formed in 1991. It made an action plan for the bird in 2008. In 2013 the group joined the Agreement on the Conservation of African-Eurasian Migratory Waterbirds
